channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Former Kenya Lionesses Coach Felix Oloo Dies in Washington at 41
item.description: Felix Oloo, famously known as 'Ade' or 'Mwalimu', the former head coach of the Kenya Lionesses, has tragically passed away at the age of 41 in Washington, USA. Oloo, who also played for Nakuru RFC for...

item.title: Xavier School Alumni Hold Golf Tournament at Wack Wack Golf and Country Club
item.description: Xavier School alumni recently organised a golf tournament held at the well-known Wack Wack Golf and Country Club, bringing together former students for a day of sport, camaraderie, and networking. Whi...

item.title: Federal budget set to boost R&D incentives while removing CGT discount worries start-ups
item.description: The upcoming federal budget is expected to introduce significant changes aimed at boosting research and development (R&D) in Australia. Treasurer Jim Chalmers plans to increase the current $150 millio...

item.title: Former OpenAI Executives Describe Internal Conflicts During Sam Altman's Leadership
item.description: During Elon Musk’s ongoing lawsuit against OpenAI, former technology chief Mira Murati testified that CEO Sam Altman caused significant internal conflict and distrust among the company's top executive...

item.title: Uber expects solid second-quarter growth despite Middle East tensions
item.description: Uber Technologies has projected higher-than-expected bookings for the second quarter, signalling resilience despite ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The San Francisco-based company attributes grow...

item.title: Garin visits DOJ but declines to confirm filing of any cases
item.description: Former or current public official Sharon Garin was seen at the Department of Justice (DOJ), drawing attention from the media and public. However, during her appearance, Garin chose not to disclose whe...

item.title: Binani Air Begins Scheduled Flights in Nigeria on May 10, 2026
item.description: Binani Air don officially open ticket sales for im maiden flight dem go start on May 10, 2026. Dis new airline talk say the move na big milestone wey go help boost the aviation sector and also create ...

item.title: Greg James’s Tandem Bike Auction Raises £11,000 for Comic Relief
item.description: BBC Radio 1 presenter Greg James completed a 1,000km (630-mile) tandem bike ride across England, Wales and Scotland to raise funds for Comic Relief. The custom-built bike, created by Derby-based Merci...

item.title: Scottish Government Rejects UK Plans to Reform Nuclear Oversight
item.description: The UK Government, led by Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer, has proposed reforms to nuclear regulation intended to streamline the development of nuclear power and weapons projects. These reforms includ...

item.title: FBI Director Criticizes Local Authorities’ Handling of Nancy Guthrie Investigation
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel has publicly criticized the Pima County Sheriff’s Department in Arizona for delaying the agency’s involvement in the search for Nancy Guthrie, the 84-year-old mother of journal...

item.title: Florida Man Faces Federal Charges for Threatening President Trump and Other Officials
item.description: Nathaniel Sanders II, a 32-year-old man from Florida, has been arrested and faces federal charges for allegedly making threats against President Donald Trump, Secretary of State Marco Rubio, and forme...

item.title: Two Suspected Motorcycle Thieves Arrested in Calamba After Student's Vehicle Stolen
item.description: Authorities in Calamba, Laguna successfully apprehended two men suspected of stealing a motorcycle belonging to a student. The incident, which raised concern in the local community, highlights the ong...

item.title: Philippines Requests Brief Access to Aung San Suu Kyi After Her Transfer to House Arrest
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) of the Philippines has formally asked Myanmar to grant Foreign Affairs Secretary Ma. Theresa Lazaro brief access to detained leader Aung San Suu Kyi. This reque...

item.title: Paraffin Price Hike Forces Hard Choices for Low-Income Families This Winter
item.description: In South Africa, inland paraffin prices surged to R28.43 per litre as of 6 May 2026, up from R24.21 a month earlier. This sharp R4.22 increase comes at the start of winter, placing a severe strain on ...

item.title: WiseTech employees face prolonged uncertainty amid AI-driven restructuring and job cuts
item.description: Workers at Australian logistics software company WiseTech Global have spent nearly three months waiting to learn whether they will be among roughly 2,000 employees set to lose their jobs, following th...

item.title: Recurring Violence Against African Migrants in South Africa
item.description: South Africa is once again facing a troubling wave of xenophobic attacks, this time targeting Nigerian nationals and other African migrants. Groups such as Operation Dudula have mobilised across multi...

item.title: Oxford Union president-elect removed following allegations of election irregularities
item.description: The Oxford Union has been thrown into turmoil after the president-elect, Catherine Xu, was removed from her position due to allegations of election fraud. Xu, a Chinese postgraduate student at Exeter ...

item.title: Claude Managed Agents Introduce Memory Preservation Through Periodic 'Dreaming'
item.description: At the Code with Claude developers' conference, Anthropic introduced a new feature for Claude Managed Agents called 'dreaming.' This process allows agents to review recent events and identify informat...

item.title: City Council debates expanding Fair Fares as enrollment challenges persist
item.description: City Council Speaker Julie Menin criticized Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s administration over the limited reach of the existing Fair Fares program, a transit discount initiative for low-income New Yorkers. M...

item.title: Supreme Court asked whether it can guide Parliament on choosing Election Commission chief
item.description: The issue revolves around whether the judiciary, particularly the Supreme Court of India, has the authority to guide Parliament on the selection process of the Chief Election Commissioner. This questi...
